title: Conditionals
description: Branch your program with if, else, and comparison operators.
objectives: Write if / else if / else statements
recap: `if / else if / else` lets your program make decisions. Comparison operators (`===`, `<`, `>`) produce booleans; logical operators (`&&`, `||`, `!`) combine them.
practicePrompts: Write a program that classifies a number as positive, negative, or zero.
Can you check if a year is a leap year? (Divisible by 4, but not 100, unless also 400.)

practiceExercises.title: Positive, negative, or zero
practiceExercises.prompt: Set `n = -5`. Print exactly one of: `positive`, `negative`, or `zero` depending on `n`. With `n = -5`, the output must be `negative`.
practiceExercises.goal: Use if / else if / else to classify a number's sign.
practiceExercises.starterCode:
const n = -5;
// Print positive / negative / zero

practiceExercises.title: Leap year check
practiceExercises.prompt: Set `year = 2024`. A leap year is divisible by 4, except it's NOT a leap year if it's divisible by 100 — unless it's also divisible by 400. Print `leap` or `not leap`. For 2024 the output should be `leap`.
hints: Combine: divisible by 4 AND (not divisible by 100, OR divisible by 400).

practiceExercises.title: One FizzBuzz value
practiceExercises.prompt: Set `n = 15`. Print `Fizz` if n is divisible by 3 but not 5, `Buzz` if divisible by 5 but not 3, `FizzBuzz` if divisible by both, and the number itself otherwise. For `n = 15` the output is `FizzBuzz`.
practiceExercises.goal: Combine conditions to cover four mutually exclusive cases.
practiceExercises.starterCode:
const n = 15;
// Print Fizz / Buzz / FizzBuzz / or n
hints: Check the combined case (divisible by both) FIRST, otherwise the single checks catch it.
`n % 15 === 0` is the same as divisible by both 3 and 5.
